evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure data promoted to the curated zone meets quality expectations; reliable, well documented, and usable datasets; keeping definitions consistent and logic reusable; standardizing key definitions
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Quality, consistency, documentation, and reliability dominate the responsibility set—data governance and definition standardization take priority over shipping speed.
evidence.domain_risk: Marketing measurement and decision-making; performance transparent and comparable across campaigns and products; enabling faster insights
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Role supports marketing and product team decisions at scale without explicit financial or compliance language, suggesting moderate consequence for errors.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and maintain the Marketing Data Model; Design and own curated datasets/marts; Build the marketing feedback loop foundation; Contribute to documentation and discoverability in DataHub
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: JD frames role as establishing data foundations and infrastructure ('starting with GA4 and Google Ads, expanding'), suggesting early-stage platform development.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build and maintain the Marketing Data Model (starting with GA4 and Google Ads, expanding as new sources and use cases appear); Design and own curated datasets/marts (stable schemas, reusable logic); Implement transformations using SQL (Redshift/Spectrum)
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical context (GA4, Google Ads, Redshift/Spectrum, stable schemas, reusable logic) shows hiring manager detail about actual analytical work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Marketing measurement and decision-making; partner with analysts, marketers, and engineers; improve data quality, evolve core metrics; support self-serve reporting; acquisition and product analytics
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary focus is serving marketing team's need for measurement, campaign performance, and self-serve analytics—commercial orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Design and own curated datasets/marts; Build and maintain the Marketing Data Model; Build the marketing feedback loop foundation; Ensure data meets quality expectations
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Role owns data model architecture, dataset design, and quality standards—clear strategic ownership of technical domain with execution in service of marketing.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools, ML infrastructure, or AI-related candidate expectations in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Ensure data promoted to the curated zone meets quality expectations; improve tracking quality
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality is framed as an owned responsibility and expectation, not just a tool or practice to be familiar with.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Ensure data promoted to the curated zone meets quality expectations; keeping definitions consistent and logic reusable; reliable, well documented datasets; standardizing key definitions
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High emphasis on data reliability, consistency, and governance to prevent bad data reaching marketing stakeholders and enabling confident decision-making.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build and maintain the Marketing Data Model (starting with GA4 and Google Ads, expanding as new sources and use cases appear); Build the marketing feedback loop foundation for automated martech systems
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Language emphasizes 'build' and 'starting with' new infrastructure, signaling platform foundation work rather than maintaining existing systems.
evidence.language_gate: Good knowledge of English and Ukrainian or Russian required
evidence.loss_aversion: Ensure data promoted to the curated zone meets quality expectations; improve tracking quality; standardizing key definitions. Role emphasizes reliable, well-documented datasets for confident decision-making.
